revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24608848913|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:航空神社]]
{{Citation needed|date=November 2019|qq=}} [[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|thumb|{{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|lt=Aviation Shrine in Minato, Tokyo|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}]] '''Kōkūjinja''' (航空神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} that uses “Aviation” as its name. All are famous for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|qid=Q640853|lt=Aviation Safety|1=Aviation safety}} and travel safety. Since the invention of the {{ill|Airplane|en|Airplane|qid=Q197|lt=Airplane|1=Airplane}}, the frequency of {{ill|Aviation Accident|ja|航空事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Accident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Aviation accident|qid=Q744913}} has increased. As a result, many—starting with military personnel—became war dead {{ill|Line of duty death|en|Line of duty death|qid=Q1036604|lt=War Dead|1=Line of duty death}}, yet their spirits were not enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. Therefore, to console these souls and honor their meritorious deeds at various units and schools, Aviation Shrines were established throughout the country. == Shrines Bearing the Name "Aviation Shrine" == * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|lt=Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|lt=Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} – Located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato Ward|1=Minato, Tokyo}} and {{ill|Shinbashi|en|Shinbashi|qid=Q1347299|lt=Shinbashi|1=Shinbashi}}, it is situated on the rooftop of the {{ill|Japan Air Lines Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Air Lines Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1770611}}’s Aviation Hall. * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Aviation Shrine|qq=}} – Located within the 1st Passenger Terminal of {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|qid=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}} (Haneda Airport) in Tokyo’s {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ota Ward|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, this shrine was founded on {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|qid=Q2701|lt=July 11|1=July 11}}, {{ill|1963|en|1963|qid=Q2755|lt=1963|1=1963}} (Showa 38) by enshrining a branch spirit {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Branch Spirit|1=Bunrei}} through {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} from the above Aviation Shrine. Formerly, the airport branch of {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}} was also relocated nearby; however, during the terminal relocation in {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}}5), it was merged into the main shrine. * Aviation Shrine – Located in Akebonochō, {{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|qid=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa City|1=Tachikawa, Tokyo}}, Tokyo. It is said to have originated when a local influential person enshrined {{ill|Kitano Tenmangu|ja|京都北野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenmangu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a ward against unlucky directions, and later, at the request of the Army Air Force’s 5th Squadron, consolidated the aviation war dead. * Aviation Shrine – Situated in Kawajiri, Midori Ward ({{ill|Sagamihara|en|Sagamihara|qid=Q209779|lt=Sagamihara City|1=Sagamihara}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}), this shrine was established to console the souls of fallen crew: on March 23, {{ill|1941|en|1941|qid=Q5231|lt=1941|1=1941}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}16) a twin-engine reconnaissance aircraft crashed and burst into flames killing two crew members, and on April 8, {{ill|1944|en|1944|qid=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Showa 19) a Japanese Army Hayabusa fighter crashed with its pilot perishing. * {{ill|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|航空神社 (所沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|1=Sanctuaire de l'aviation (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124483190}} – Located at the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|lt_dep=北野天神社|lt_ja=北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|1=Kitano Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404276}} in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} {{ill|Tokorozawa, Saitama|en|Tokorozawa, Saitama|qid=Q165857|lt=Tokorozawa|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, in the area of {{ill|Kotesashi Motomachi|ja|小手指元町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotesashi Motomachi|1=Kotesashi Motomachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11460790}}. It was founded on September 25, Showa 12 by fulfilling the wish of {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|qid=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|1=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}}, who, having received sacred wood from the construction of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingu|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} and materials from {{ill|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|en|Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|qid=Q703633|lt=Hikawa Shrine|1=Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)}}, built a shrine at the southern end of the {{ill|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|ja|所沢陸軍飛行場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Army Airfield|1=Tokorozawa Airfield|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658682}} to enshrine {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and to conduct ceremonies for the aviation war dead. At that time, because aviation war dead were not enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}, they were venerated as protective deities of the nation. In Showa 13, the shrine was transferred to the premises of the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy|en|Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy|qid=Q2822344|lt=Army Aviation Officer School|1=Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy}} established in Toyooka (now part of the Japan Air Self-Defense Force’s {{ill|Iruma Air Base|en|Iruma Air Base|qid=Q3015843|lt=Iruma Base|1=Iruma Air Base}}), becoming the center of reverence for its students. When the school grounds were seized by the U.S. military at the end of the war in Showa 20, aviation officials led by the school’s head {{ill|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|en|Yoshitoshi Tokugawa|qid=Q5694703|lt=Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|1=Yoshitoshi Tokugawa}} petitioned to preserve the shrine permanently. With the cooperation of local volunteers and the chief priest, Ryōsuke Kurihara, of the long-serving {{ill|Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|lt_dep=北野天神社|lt_ja=北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|1=Kitano Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404276}}, the enshrinement was transferred in its original form on September 3, Showa 20, to the current {{ill|Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|lt_dep=北野天神社|lt_ja=北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenmangu (Tokorozawa)|1=Kitano Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404276}}. Ceremonies have continued unceasingly ever since, with visits from local citizens, Prince Higashikuni, the governor of Saitama Prefecture, the mayor of Tokorozawa, successive heads of the Army Aviation Officer School, and Japan’s first official domestic flight pioneer, Tokugawa Yoshitoshi. After the establishment of the Air Self-Defense Force in 1954 (Showa 29), war dead personnel were enshrined at this Aviation Shrine (with branch enshrinements following the construction of the Defense Agency Memorial Monument). On November 19, {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} (Showa 40), the spirit tablet was transferred to the Air Self-Defense Force Candidate School, then on February 26, {{ill|1988|en|1988|qid=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} (Showa 63) to the Air Self-Defense Force Iruma Base Shūbudai Memorial Hall. On March 13, {{ill|2024|en|2024|qid=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}} (Reiwa 6), the spirit tablet was again transferred and enshrined at Kitano Tenmangu, and from April 13, {{ill|2024|en|2024|qid=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}} (Reiwa 6) onward, the issuance of goshuin and aviation safety charms began. * {{ill|Museum of Aeronautical Science|en|Museum of Aeronautical Science|qid=Q11613905|lt=Aviation Science Museum|1=Museum of Aeronautical Science}} – Located within the premises of the Aviation Science Museum in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Sanbu District|en|Sanbu District|qid=Q1153740|lt=Sanbu District|1=Sanbu District}}, {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|en|Shibayama, Chiba|qid=Q1203689|lt=Shibayama Town|1=Shibayama, Chiba}}, and {{ill|Iwayama|ja|岩山 (芝山町)|lt_dep=岩山|lt_ja=岩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayama|1=Iwayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q47392910}}, this shrine was originally established in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Showa 12) by aviation pioneer {{ill|Otojirô Itô|en|Otojirô Itô|qid=Q11380502|lt=Itō Otojirō|1=Otojirô Itô}} to enshrine the war dead of the early aviation era—including his eldest son Shintarō and the celebrated aviator {{ill|Yamagata Toyotaro|ja|山縣豊太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Toyotaro|1=Yamagata Toyotaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110774379}}—at the factory premises ({{ill|Factory|en|Factory|qid=Q83405|lt=Factory|1=Factory}} and {{ill|Premises|ja|敷地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Premises|1=site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499678}}) of {{ill|Itō Aircraft Manufacturing|ja|伊藤飛行機研究所|lt_dep=Itō Aircraft Factory|lt_ja=伊藤飛行機製作所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Aircraft Manufacturing|1=Itō Aircraft Manufacturing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380505}} in Tsudanuma, {{ill|Chiba District, Chiba|en|Chiba District, Chiba|qid=Q5095301|lt=Chiba District|1=Chiba District, Chiba}}’s Tsudanuma Town (now part of {{ill|Narashino|en|Narashino|qid=Q318047|lt=Narashino|1=Narashino}}) in Saginuma. Following Japan’s defeat ({{ill|Surrender of Japan|en|Surrender of Japan|qid=Q6540361|lt=Japan's War Defeat|1=Surrender of Japan}}), Itō, forced out of the aviation industry, became a farmer in the eastern peak area of Toyama Village (now part of Narita City) in {{ill|Toyama Village|ja|遠山村 (千葉県)|lt_dep=遠山村|lt_ja=遠山村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyama Village|1=Tōyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642044}} through postwar development ({{ill|Postwar Development|ja|戦後開拓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Postwar Development|1=Postwar Development|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11496073}}). After some time, the local community decided to build a shrine, and on November 23, {{ill|1953|en|1953|qid=Q18585|lt=1953|1=1953}} (Showa 28) the shrine was relocated. At that time, a new enshrinement of {{ill|Ninomiya Sontoku|en|Ninomiya Sontoku|qid=Q968964|lt=Ninomiya Sontoku|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} was made from {{ill|Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine|en|Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine|qid=Q11428033|lt=Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine|1=Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine}} of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. Later, the shrine was renamed “Tōhō Shrine” and came to be revered by the local settlers as the guardian shrine ({{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}}) of their new community. However, with the construction of the new Tokyo International Airport (now {{ill|Narita International Airport|en|Narita International Airport|qid=Q36454|lt=Narita International Airport|1=Narita International Airport}}), in September 2001 (Heisei 13) at the request of Itō Otojirō’s family, the spirit of Ninomiya Sontoku was retained and transferred to the outdoor display area of the Aviation Science Museum in Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. The shrine’s {{ill|Omamori|en|Omamori|qid=Q1756788|lt=Amulet|1=Omamori}} is available at the museum’s shop. * {{ill|Japan Aviation High School|en|Japan Aviation High School|qid=Q576350|lt=Japan Air High School|1=Japan Aviation High School}} – Located in Utsutani, {{ill|Kai, Yamanashi|en|Kai, Yamanashi|qid=Q577309|lt=Kai City|1=Kai, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, this shrine is enshrined within the campus of Japan Air High School. * {{ill|Japan Air High School Ishikawa|ja|日本航空高等学校石川|lt_dep=Aviation Shrine (Noto)|lt_ja=能登航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Air High School Ishikawa|1=Japan Aviation High School Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11509002}} – Located in Suuei, Mitsuimachi, {{ill|Wajima, Ishikawa|en|Wajima, Ishikawa|qid=Q823896|lt=Wajima City|1=Wajima, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, this shrine is enshrined within the campus of Japan Air High School Ishikawa. * {{ill|Senshu Aviation Shrine|ja|泉州航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshu Aviation Shrine|1=Senshu Aviation Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17995313}} – Located in Kamikawaya, {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|qid=Q852018|lt=Izumisano|1=Izumisano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, this shrine was established by branching off from the {{ill|Iwafune Shrine|ja|磐船神社 (交野市)|lt_dep=磐船神社|lt_ja=磐船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwafune Shrine|1=Iwafune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587926}} of the private municipality in {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}}, on the occasion of constructing {{ill|Kansai International Airport|en|Kansai International Airport|qid=Q193682|lt=Kansai International Airport|1=Kansai International Airport}}. It is dedicated to prayers for aviation safety and travel safety; its official name is “Senshu Iwafune Shrine.” * {{ill|Takayama Aviation Shrine|ja|高山航空公園|lt_dep=Takayama Aviation Shrine|lt_ja=高山航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Aviation Shrine|1=Takayama Kouku Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11669430}} – Located in Higashibun Otsu, {{ill|Ayauta District, Kagawa|en|Ayauta District, Kagawa|qid=Q1123066|lt=Ayauta District|1=Ayauta District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Ayagawa, Kagawa|en|Ayagawa, Kagawa|qid=Q1156043|lt=Ayagawa Town|1=Ayagawa, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, this shrine was founded on December 16, {{ill|1990|en|1990|qid=Q2064|lt=1990|1=1990}} (Heisei 2) in conjunction with the development of Takayama Aviation Park, prompted by airplanes flying over after the completion of {{ill|Takamatsu Airport|en|Takamatsu Airport|qid=Q1334603|lt=Takamatsu Airport|1=Takamatsu Airport}}, as a prayer for the safety of air transportation. Next to the shrine stand an Army Youth Aviator Memorial Monument, established by volunteers from Kagawa Prefecture, and an ancient local earth god’s shrine. Since its enshrinement, successive {{ill|Minister of Transport|ja|運輸大臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Transport|1=Minister of Transport of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11640486}}s and {{ill|Minister of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|en|Minister of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|qid=Q842012|lt=Minister of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|1=Minister of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} have visited, with commemorative tree plantings around it. == Shrines Closely Associated with Aviation == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|1=Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646485}} – Located in {{ill|Toranomon|en|Toranomon|qid=Q7825412|lt=Toranomon|1=Toranomon}}, Minato Ward, Tokyo, it was established when {{ill|Kyogoku Takakazu|ja|京極高和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyogoku Takakazu|1=Kyōgoku Takakazu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374457}}—the feudal lord of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} and the {{ill|Marugame Domain|en|Marugame Domain|qid=Q10863480|lt=Marugame Domain|1=Marugame Domain}}—enshrined {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Daigongen|1=Kotohira-gū}} at his Edo residence. * {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}} – Located in {{ill|Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo|en|Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q1494212|lt=Haneda|1=Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo}}, Ota Ward, Tokyo. The area now occupied by {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|qid=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}} (specifically, {{ill|Haneda Airport|ja|羽田空港 (大田区)|lt_dep=羽田空港|lt_ja=羽田空港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Airport|1=Haneda Airport|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610321}}) was once part of the grounds and monzen town of this shrine until {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Showa 20). Even before the opening of {{ill|Tokyo Airfield|ja|東京飛行場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Airfield|1=Tokyo Airfield|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q128845377}} in {{ill|1931|en|1931|qid=Q18782|lt=1931|1=1931}} (Showa 6), during the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taisho Era|1=Taishō era}}, there is an account of flight school trainees in the monzen praying for safety. It remains the shrine nearest to the airport and was originally founded to protect Haneda Airport ({{ill|Suzuki Shinden|ja|鈴木新田 (大田区)|lt_dep=鈴木新田|lt_ja=鈴木新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzuki Shinden|qq=}}) from disaster. As an airport-protecting shrine, it attracts prayers for safety from the aviation industry, related government agencies, and travelers. Additionally, on the roof of the old Haneda Airport Terminal Building (prior to offshore expansion), both Haneda Aviation Shrine and the Anamori Inari Shrine Airport Branch were enshrined; even now, branch spirits are maintained on the grounds of the {{ill|Japan Airlines|en|Japan Airlines|qid=Q213140|lt=Japan Air Lines|1=Japan Airlines}} Main Maintenance Center and the {{ill|San-Ai Oil|en|San-Ai Oil|qid=Q7413196|lt=Sanei Oburi|1=San-Ai Oil}} Haneda branch. {{Main|穴守稲荷神社#羽田空港（航空業界）との関係|qq=}} * {{ill|Hamakū Shrine|ja|雷神社 (横須賀市)|lt_dep=浜空神社|lt_ja=浜空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamakū Shrine|1=Ikazuchi Shrine(Yokosuka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11660191}} – Located in {{ill|Yokosuka|en|Yokosuka|qid=Q200578|lt=Yokosuka City|1=Yokosuka}}’s {{ill|Oihama Honmachi|ja|追浜本町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oihama Honmachi|1=Oppama-honchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122148344}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. In {{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} (Showa 13), following the establishment of the {{ill|Yokohama Air Group|en|Yokohama Air Group|qid=Q8054612|lt=Yokohama Navy Air Corps|1=Yokohama Air Group}} in Tomioka, Kanazawa Ward, Yokohama, the {{ill|Iseyama Ōdai Shrine|ja|伊勢山皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iseyama Ōdai Shrine|1=Iseyama Kōtai Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378990}} was enshrined to create Torifune Shrine. After being seized by the U.S. military post-war and released in {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Showa 46), the comrades association “Hamakū-kai” restored the damaged Torifune Shrine and, as “Hamakū Shrine,” held memorial services for fallen members. Eventually, due to aging, the association relocated the shrine building to {{ill|Yokosuka Raijin Shrine|ja|横須賀雷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokosuka Raijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503394|1=Yokosuka Raijin Shrine}} in {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20) and erected a memorial monument inscribed with “Rest in Peace, Navy Seaplane Corps” on the former site. * {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine (as associated with Yasukuni)|ja|深見神社|lt_dep=靖國社|lt_ja=靖國社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine (as associated with Yasukuni)|1=Fukami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11560278}} – Located in Fukami, {{ill|Yamato, Kanagawa|en|Yamato, Kanagawa|qid=Q386697|lt=Yamato City|1=Yamato, Kanagawa}}, Kanagawa Prefecture, this shrine is the subsidiary of {{ill|Fukami Shrine|ja|深見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukami Shrine|1=Fukami Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11560278}}. It was once enshrined within the {{ill|Atsugi Navy Air Corps Base|ja|厚木海軍航空隊|lt_dep=厚木海軍航空隊基地|lt_ja=厚木海軍航空隊基地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsugi Navy Air Corps Base|1=Atsugi Navy Air Corps Base|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11409262}} (currently {{ill|Atsugi Base|ja|厚木基地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsugi Base|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厚木海軍飛行場|1=Naval Air Facility Atsugi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3016710}}) and was known as Atsugi Air Shrine, enshrining the war dead register of the Atsugi Navy Air Corps along with a treasured sword bestowed by Yasukuni Shrine. * {{ill|Tōhō Shrine|ja|東峰神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Shrine|1=Tōhō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526776}} – Located in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|qid=Q273798|lt=Narita City|1=Narita, Chiba}}’s {{ill|Tōhō|ja|東峰 (成田市)|lt_dep=東峰|lt_ja=東峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō|1=Tōhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q47392901}}, Chiba Prefecture, it is set within the grounds of the Aviation Science Museum, where Ninomiya Sontoku was enshrined alongside the spirit of the Aviation Shrine. In {{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}} (Heisei 13), the spirit of the Aviation Shrine was transferred to the museum’s outdoor display area in {{ill|Iwayama|ja|岩山 (芝山町)|lt_dep=岩山|lt_ja=岩山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayama|1=Iwayama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q47392910}}, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. * Hyakuri Shrine – Located in Yamano, {{ill|Omitama|en|Omitama|qid=Q430584|lt=Komidama City|1=Omitama}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, it originally served as the guardian deity shrine for the Imperial Navy’s {{ill|Hyakuri Naval Air Corps|ja|百里原海軍航空隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuri Naval Air Corps|1=Hyakuri Naval Air Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580868}} (linked with {{ill|Imperial Japanese Navy|en|Imperial Japanese Navy|qid=Q219712|lt=Imperial Navy|1=Imperial Japanese Navy}}). * {{ill|Azumi Shrine|ja|安住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Shrine|1=Yasuzumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450263}} – Situated in Kamitakanezawa, {{ill|Takanezawa, Tochigi|en|Takanezawa, Tochigi|qid=Q648859|lt=Takanezawa Town|1=Takanezawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Shioya District, Tochigi|en|Shioya District, Tochigi|qid=Q1346028|lt=Shioya District|1=Shioya District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, its grounds feature a {{ill|Heliport|en|Heliport|qid=Q502074|lt=Helipad|1=Heliport}} ({{ill|Out-of-field Landing/Takeoff Area|ja|場外離着陸場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Out-of-field Landing/Takeoff Area|1=off-site landing site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11428082}}). * {{ill|Isesaki Shrine|ja|伊勢崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isesaki Shrine|1=Isesaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11379009}} – Located in {{ill|Honchō|ja|本町 (伊勢崎市)|lt_dep=本町|lt_ja=本町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō|1=Honmachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86736629}}, {{ill|Isesaki|en|Isesaki|qid=Q328596|lt=Isesaki City|1=Isesaki}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, this shrine houses a propeller that was dedicated by employees of {{ill|Nakajima Aircraft Company|en|Nakajima Aircraft Company|qid=Q1476227|lt=Nakashima Aircraft|1=Nakajima Aircraft Company}} (later Fuji Heavy Industries, now {{ill|Subaru Corporation|en|Subaru Corporation|qid=Q1051297|lt=SUBARU|1=Subaru Corporation}}) in prayer for safe returns from the battlefield. This propeller has influenced the design of goshuin and amulets for travel and aviation safety. * Toriya Shrine – Located in Hagurochō, {{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|qid=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki City|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, it is famous for its goshuin book associated with the {{ill|Blue Impulse|en|Blue Impulse|qid=Q2505817|lt=Blue Impulse|1=Blue Impulse}}. * {{ill|Shimomasuda Shrine|ja|下増田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomasuda Shrine|1=Shimomasuda Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11360748}} – Situated at the Shimomasuda Mansion in {{ill|Natori, Miyagi|en|Natori, Miyagi|qid=Q642018|lt=Natori City|1=Natori, Miyagi}}, Miyagi Prefecture, it enshrines an aviation shrine originally established in {{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}} (Showa 14) at the former {{ill|Sendai Army Flying School|ja|仙台陸軍飛行学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Army Flying School|1=Sendai Army Flying School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378081}}. * {{ill|Hatsukuni Shrine|ja|肇國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsukuni Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 溝旗神社|1=Mizobata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564497}} – Located in Mizonokibacho, {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, this shrine is a subsidiary of {{ill|Mizonokiba Shrine|ja|溝旗神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizonokiba Shrine|1=Mizobata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564497}}. It was founded on November 13, {{ill|1944|en|1944|qid=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Showa 19) within the Second Education Unit of the {{ill|Gifu Army Aviation Maintenance School|ja|岐阜陸軍航空整備学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Army Aviation Maintenance School|1=Gifu Army Aviation Maintenance School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471899}}, enshrined as the guardian for students of aviation technology, later transferred to the grounds of Mizonokiba Shrine, and eventually merged with the spirits of the school’s graduates (boy pilots). * {{ill|Asahi Inari Shrine|ja|朝日稲荷神社 (各務原市)|lt_dep=朝日稲荷神社|lt_ja=朝日稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Inari Shrine|1=Asahi Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q112336630}} – Located in {{ill|Kakamigahara|en|Kakamigahara|qid=Q641491|lt=Kakamigahara City|1=Kakamigahara}}’s {{ill|Unuma Asahimachi|ja|鵜沼朝日町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unuma Asahimachi|1=Unuma Asahimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131870043}}, Gifu Prefecture, this shrine was established as an aviation shrine at the Imperial Japanese Army’s base (displayed as {{ill|Gifu Air Field|en|Gifu Air Field|qid=Q3275507|lt=Gifu Base|1=Gifu Air Field}}) and was later renamed when relocated after the war. * {{ill|Kuga Shrine|ja|久我神社 (京都市北区)|lt_dep=久我神社|lt_ja=久我神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuga Shrine|1=Kuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369376}} – Located in Shichikitanakedera-chō, {{ill|Kita-ku, Kyoto|en|Kita-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154970|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, this shrine is an affiliate of {{ill|Kamigamo Shrine|en|Kamigamo Shrine|qid=Q700448|lt=Kamobetsu Raijin Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and enshrines Kamo Tatemune of the {{ill|Kamo clan|en|Kamo clan|qid=Q2975287|lt=Kamo Clan|1=Kamo clan}} (associated with {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|qid=Q745463|lt=Yatagarasu|1=Yatagarasu}}). It is especially venerated for conferring benefits in “traffic safety” and “aviation safety.” * {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}} – Located in Hachiman Doi, {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata City|1=Yawata}}, Kyoto Prefecture, it was founded in {{ill|1915|en|1915|qid=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taisho 4) by {{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|en|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|lt=Ninomiya Tada-hachi|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}}, the first in Japan to study the principles of flight. Pained by the frequent aviation accidents since the invention of the airplane, he felt responsible for memorializing the victims of such tragedies and invested his personal funds to enshrine their spirits. It now attracts many visitors from the aviation and aerospace sectors seeking safety prayers. * {{ill|Iwafune Shrine|ja|磐船神社 (交野市)|lt_dep=磐船神社|lt_ja=磐船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwafune Shrine|1=Iwafune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587926}} – Located in the private municipality of {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano City|1=Katano}}, Osaka Prefecture, this shrine enshrines the god of airplanes, {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|lt_dep=饒速日命|lt_ja=饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, and also houses the enshrined spirits of aviation disaster victims ({{ill|Enshrinement Together|ja|合祀|1=co-Enshrinement|2=ja|3=合祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Together|13=User:Immanuelle/co-Enshrinement|qq=}}). * {{ill|Yatadazaku Shitamahiko Shrine|ja|矢田坐久志玉比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatadazaku Shitamahiko Shrine|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584205}} – Situated in Yata Town, {{ill|Yamatokōriyama|en|Yamatokōriyama|qid=Q118213|lt=Yamato-Koriyama|1=Yamatokōriyama}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, the deity enshrined here, Kushidama Nigihayahi, is revered as the progenitor of aviation based on the legend recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, which tells of him riding on a heavenly Iwafune and flying through the sky. An aviation festival is held every September 20, and a dedicated propeller hangs from the loft of the gatehouse. * Kamikaze Shrine – Located in {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|qid=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City|1=Nishinomiya}}’s {{ill|Kamigahara Yamada Town|ja|上ケ原山田町|lt_dep=上ケ原|lt_ja=上ケ原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigahara Yamada Town|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上ケ原|1=Uegahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122451456}}, Hyōgo Prefecture, it once served as the in-house shrine of the {{ill|Nishinomiya Navy Air Corps|ja|西ノ宮海軍航空隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Navy Air Corps|1=Nishinomiya Navy Air Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627661}}. Before the post-war incineration, local residents risked their lives to relocate it into {{ill|Kamigahara Hachiman Shrine|ja|上ケ原八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigahara Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503396|1=Kamigahara Hachiman Shrine}}. * {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} – Located in Kawanishi, Kotohira Town, {{ill|Nakatado District, Kagawa|en|Nakatado District, Kagawa|qid=Q1206155|lt=Nakatado District|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, it is revered as the god of safe navigation and maritime protection, with its veneration later extending to land, sea, and aviation traffic safety. * Ninomiya Flying Shrine – Situated in {{ill|Mannō, Kagawa|en|Mannō, Kagawa|qid=Q1350204|lt=Mannou Town|1=Mannō, Kagawa}}, Nakatado District, Kagawa Prefecture, at Oitsugi near {{ill|Mononoki Pass|ja|樅ノ木峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononoki Pass|1=Mominoki Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24876464}}, it was founded in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} (Heisei 3) at the site where Tada-hachi is said to have first conceived the principles of flight. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Railway Shrine|ja|鉄道神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Railway Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Japan Air Lines Association Aviation Shrine] * [https://tokyo-haneda.com/enjoy/recommended_place/index.html Haneda Aviation Shrine] – Official website of the Haneda Airport Passenger Terminal * [https://anamori.jp/ Tokyo Haneda Anamori Inari Shrine] * [https://kitanotenjin.com/kouku.html Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa, Saitama Prefecture)] * [https://www.hikoujinjya.com/ Hikō Shrine] * [https://www.senshuiwafune.jp/ Senshu Iwafune Shrine] {{disambiguation}}
{{translated page|ja|航空神社|version=103167669|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:航空神社]]
<!--Aviation Shrines-->
[[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Aviation Shrines''' (航空神社, Kōkū Jinja) are shrines named after "aviation." All of them are famous for prayers for {{ill|Aviation safety|en|Aviation safety|qid=Q640853|lt=Aviation safety|1=Aviation safety}} and travel safety.
Since the invention of [[:en:airplanes|airplanes]], [[:en:aviation accidents|aviation accidents]] have become frequent, resulting in the loss of many lives, including military personnel. However, the spirits of these individuals were not enshrined at [[Yasukuni Shrines]]. Therefore, various aviation shrines were established nationwide to honor the spirits of the deceased and commemorate their achievements.
== Shrines Named "Aviation Shrine" == * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|lt=Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|lt=Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} - Located in [[:en:Shimbashi|Shimbashi]], {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, on the rooftop of the Aviation Hall of the {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1770611}}. * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Aviation Shrine|qq=}} - Located within the [[:en:Tokyo International Airport|Tokyo International Airport]] (Haneda Airport) Terminal 1 in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q217234|lt=Ōta, Tokyo|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}. It was founded on [[July 11, 1963]] (Shōwa 38) by enshrining part of the spirit from the aforementioned Aviation Shrine. The former [[Anamori Inari Shrines]] airport branch shrine was previously located adjacent but was merged into the main shrine in 1993 (Heisei 5) during the terminal relocation. * Aviation Shrine (Tachikawa, Tokyo) - Located in [[Akebono-cho]], {{ill|Tachikawa, Tokyo|en|Tachikawa, Tokyo|qid=Q269634|lt=Tachikawa, Tokyo|1=Tachikawa, Tokyo}}. Initially, this was a [[Kimon]]-warding shrine for local influential figures, established with Kyoto's Kitano Tenmangu deity. Later, it was adapted to honor aviation martyrs due to the request of the Army Aviation 5th Squadron. * Aviation Shrine (Midori, Sagamihara, Kanagawa) - Located in [[Kawashiri]], {{ill|Midori-ku|en|Midori-ku|qid=Q1140937|lt=Midori-ku|1=Midori-ku}}, Sagamihara. It was built to memorialize two fatalities from a reconnaissance aircraft crash in 1941 (Shōwa 16) and a [[Nakajima Ki-43 "Hayabusa" (Oscar)|Hayabusa fighter aircraft]] pilot's death in 1944 (Shōwa 19). * {{ill|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa, Saitama)|ja|航空神社 (所沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa, Saitama)|1=Sanctuaire de l'aviation (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124483190}} - Located in the {{ill|Kitano Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|北野天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Tenjin Shrine (Tokorozawa)|1=Kitano Tenjinsha (Tokorozawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404275}} in [[:en:Tokorozawa|Tokorozawa]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. Founded in 1937 (Shōwa 12), it was later transferred to the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy|en|Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy|qid=Q2822344|lt=Army Aviation School|1=Imperial Japanese Army Air Academy}} before being moved back post-war. The shrine now offers aviation safety {{ill|Omamori|en|Omamori|qid=Q1756788|lt=Omamori|1=Omamori}} and goshuin (stamps). * {{ill|Aviation Shrine at the Museum of Aeronautical Science|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aviation Shrine at the Museum of Aeronautical Science|1=Aviation Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30319396}} - Located at the {{ill|Museum of Aeronautical Science|en|Museum of Aeronautical Science|qid=Q11613905|lt=Museum of Aeronautical Science|1=Museum of Aeronautical Science}} in {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|en|Shibayama, Chiba|qid=Q1203689|lt=Shibayama, Chiba|1=Shibayama, Chiba}}. Initially established by aviation pioneer {{ill|Otojirô Itô|en|Otojirô Itô|qid=Q11380502|lt=Otojirō Itō|1=Otojirô Itô}}, it memorializes aviation martyrs from the early aviation era. * {{ill|Kai Aviation Shrine|ja|甲斐航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Aviation Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503398|1=Kai Aviation Shrine}} - Located in {{ill|Kai, Yamanashi|en|Kai, Yamanashi|qid=Q577309|lt=Kai, Yamanashi|1=Kai, Yamanashi}}, within the campus of {{ill|Japan Aviation High School|en|Japan Aviation High School|qid=Q576350|lt=Japan Aviation Academy|1=Japan Aviation High School}}. * {{ill|Noto Aviation Shrine|ja|能登航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noto Aviation Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503400|1=Noto Aviation Shrine}} - Located in {{ill|Wajima, Ishikawa|en|Wajima, Ishikawa|qid=Q823896|lt=Wajima, Ishikawa|1=Wajima, Ishikawa}}, within {{ill|Japan Aviation Academy Ishikawa|ja|日本航空高等学校石川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aviation Academy Ishikawa|1=Japan Aviation High School Ishikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11509002}}. * {{ill|Senshū Aviation Shrine|ja|泉州航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshū Aviation Shrine|1=Senshu Aviation Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17995313}} - Located in [[:en:Izumisano, Osaka|Izumisano, Osaka]], established with the spirit transferred from {{ill|Iwane Shrine (Katano)|ja|磐船神社 (交野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwane Shrine (Katano)|1=Iwafune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587926}} to commemorate aviation safety. * {{ill|Takayama Aviation Shrine|ja|高山航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Aviation Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高山航空公園|1=Takayama Kouku Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11669430}} - Located in {{ill|Ayagawa, Kagawa|en|Ayagawa, Kagawa|qid=Q1156043|lt=Ayagawa, Kagawa|1=Ayagawa, Kagawa}}, established in 1990 (Heisei 2) to pray for aviation safety after the completion of Takamatsu Airport.
== Shrines Closely Associated with Aviation == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Toranomon)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (東京都港区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Toranomon)|1=Konpira Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646485}} - Located in {{ill|Toranomon|en|Toranomon|qid=Q7825412|lt=Toranomon|1=Toranomon}}, {{ill|Minato, Tokyo|en|Minato, Tokyo|qid=Q190088|lt=Minato, Tokyo|1=Minato, Tokyo}}, with historical ties to maritime and subsequently aviation safety. * {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}} - Located in {{ill|Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo|en|Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo|qid=Q1494212|lt=Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo|1=Haneda, Ōta, Tokyo}}. It remains a symbol of safety and protection for Haneda Airport. * {{ill|Hamakū Shrine|ja|雷神社 (横須賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamakū Shrine|1=Ikazuchi Shrine(Yokosuka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11660191}} - Located in [[Oppama, Yokosuka]], it honors the aviation martyrs of the [[Yokosuka Naval Air Corps]]. * {{ill|Koganei Shrine|ja|肇國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 溝旗神社|1=Mizobata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564497}} - Located in [[:en:Gifu City|Gifu City]], it once served as the guardian shrine for the Gifu Army Aviation Maintenance School. * {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata|1=Yawata}}, established by aviation pioneer {{ill|Chūhachi Ninomiya|en|Chūhachi Ninomiya|qid=Q3655885|lt=Chūhachi Ninomiya|1=Chūhachi Ninomiya}}.
== Related Articles == * [[Railway Shrines]]
